title: Earnings call transcript: Riot Platforms Q1 2026 revenue beats, stock rises
text: Riot Platforms reported Q1 2026 earnings with revenue of $167 million, beating the forecast of $122.26 million. The company posted a net loss of $500 million or $1.44 per share. The stock price rose by 7.7% during regular trading and a further 2% in aftermarket trading. Riot booked its first meaningful data center contribution at $33.2 million, in addition to core Bitcoin mining revenue. Riot produced 1,473 bitcoin in Q1 and ended the quarter with a deployed hashrate of 42.5 exahash. The data center segment is becoming increasingly important to overall profitability. Riot anticipates exiting 2026 with an annualized operating lease revenue run rate of $37.8 million, scaling to $55.6 million by end of 2027. The company is targeting 80% plus NOI margins for the data center segment as site operations mature and AMD scales into its full 50 megawatt footprint. AMD exercised an option to double its contracted data center capacity from 25 MW to 50 MW. The company projects ongoing revenue growth with anticipated expansions in both data center and Bitcoin mining segments, with continued emphasis on cost reduction and strategic capacity expansions.
